reasoning: The informal claim "ℚ has no proper subgroups of finite index" is equivalent to "every proper (additive) subgroup of ℚ has infinite index." In Mathlib, `AddSubgroup.index H = Nat.card (ℚ ⧸ H)`, and `Nat.card` returns 0 exactly when the set is infinite, so `H.index = 0` means H has infinite index. The hypothesis `H ≠ ⊤` correctly encodes "proper subgroup." Thus the Lean signature quantifies over all proper additive subgroups and concludes infinite index, matching the informal statement exactly.
